thm:1.summary: Conditional bounded gaps: under primes-level-of-distribution $\theta > 1/2$, every admissible $k$-tuple with $k \ge C(\theta)$ contains at least 2 primes infinitely often. Specifically $\theta \ge 0.971 \Rightarrow k \ge 6$ works (gives $H_1 \le 16$ under EH).

thm:2.summary: $E_1 := \liminf_n (p_{n+1} - p_n) / \log p_n = 0$. Unconditional. The qualitative breakthrough that small gaps exist on average — the result that started the entire bounded-gaps program.

thm:3.summary: Quantitative $E_r \le (\sqrt{r} - \sqrt{2\theta})^2$ conditional on level-of-distribution $\theta$, $E_r \le (\sqrt{r} - 1)^2$ unconditional. The $m$-th gap version of thm:2.

prop:1.summary: $S_1$-type asymptotic: $\sum \Lambda_R(n;\mathcal{H}_1,\ell_1)\,\Lambda_R(n;\mathcal{H}_2,\ell_2) = \binom{\ell_1+\ell_2}{\ell_1} \frac{(\log R)^{r+\ell_1+\ell_2}}{(r+\ell_1+\ell_2)!} (\mathfrak{S}(\mathcal{H}) + o(1)) N$. The sieve weight squared sum — direct ancestor of Maynard's prop:4.1 $S_1$.

prop:2.summary: $S_2$-type asymptotic: weighted prime count $\sum \Lambda_R^2(n)\,\theta(n+h_0)$ — direct ancestor of Maynard's prop:4.1 $S_2$. The bridge from sieve to primes.

lem:1.summary: Contour integral bounds for $\int_{\mathcal{L}} (\log(|s|+3))^B |R^s/s^k\,ds|$. Analytic input feeding the asymptotic evaluation in prop:1, prop:2.

lem:2.summary: Divisor sum bounds: $\sum_{q \le x} d_m(q)/q \le (\lceil m \rceil + \log x)^{\lceil m \rceil}$. Used in error term control.

lem:3.summary: Perturbed divisor-correlation asymptotic $\mathcal{T}^*_R(a,b,d,u,v,h)$. Generalizes prop:1/prop:2 to handle shifts $h \ll R^C$ — the technical heart of the GPY argument.

edges.note: $S_1$-type evaluation needed for the sieve ratio.
Same machinery, taking $k \to \infty$ to drive the ratio to zero.
Prime count blow-up of the sieve ratio.
$m$-th gap variant uses the same sieve framework.
Quantitative version using $S_2$ with multiple primes captured.
Contour-integral evaluation of the divisor sum.
Same contour evaluation with the prime indicator.
Shifted $\mathcal{T}^*_R$ asymptotic is the technical lemma carrying the prime indicator.
Shifted $\mathcal{T}^*_R$ asymptotic also feeds the unshifted case.

main_results.description: thm:1 (conditional bounded gaps under EH-like $\theta > 1/2$), thm:2 (unconditional $E_1 = 0$), thm:3 (quantitative $m$-th gap bound).

selberg_sieve_engine.description: prop:1 / prop:2: 1D Selberg sieve sums $S_1, S_2$ in closed form. Direct ancestor of Maynard's $k$-dimensional prop:4.1.

analytic_machinery.description: lem:1 (contour integral), lem:2 (divisor-function bounds), lem:3 (perturbed $\mathcal{T}^*_R$ asymptotic). Standard tools that feed the Selberg sums.

ElliottHalberstam:1970.title: A conjecture in prime number theory

HardyLittlewood:1923.title: Some problems of 'Partitio numerorum' III: On the expression of a number as a sum of primes
HardyLittlewood:1923.role: foundational — prime $k$-tuples conjecture and singular series
